sections.paragraph: Chald. f. Dan 3:5, and שַׂבְּכָא verses , 10 , 15 sambuca, Greek σαμβύκη, a musical instrument with strings similar to the nablium (compare נֶבֶל); see Athen. iv. 23, p. 175; xvi. 8, p. 633 and 9, P. 637; Casaub. Strabo x. p. 471; Casaub. Vitruv. vi. 1, x. 22. And Strabo indeed, loc. cit. says that the Greek word is of barbarous, i.e. oriental origin; in that case it may be so called from the interweaving of the strings (root סָבַךְ); in Dan. loc. cit. it is connected with סוּמְפֹּנְיָה, a word clearly of Greek origin.
